questions.question: India won ICC Women’s Cricket World Cup 2025 by defeating which country in the final?
questions.explanation: On 2nd November 2025, India’s Women’s Cricket Team won its first-ever ICC Women’s ODI World Cup title, defeating South Africa by 52 runs at Dr. DY Patil Sports Academy, Navi Mumbai. The ICC Women’s ODI World Cup 2025 was held from September 30 to November 2, 2025. Shefali Verma (21 years) was named Player of the Match. Deepti Sharma became Player of the Tournament.

questions.question: Who has become the first Indian female to hold 11 Guinness World Records in freestyle skating?
questions.explanation: Janvi Jindal, 18, from Chandigarh has become the first Indian female to hold 11 Guinness World Records in freestyle skating. She recently added six new records in November, including the most 360-degree rotations on inline skates in 30 seconds and one minute, and most one-wheeled 360-degree spins. Janvi earlier earned five Guinness titles in July 2025. She is now India’s second-highest Guinness record holder, after Sachin Tendulkar (19 records). Janvi is self-trained and also holds 21 total records, including India Book, Asia Book and Worldwide Book records. Chandigarh University honoured her with a ₹11,000 award and sports scholarship.

questions.question: Which country won the Women’s Kabaddi World Cup 2025?
questions.explanation: India won the Women’s Kabaddi World Cup 2025 defeating Chinese Taipei 35–28 in the final held in Dhaka, Bangladesh. This is India’s second consecutive title showing strong dominance in women’s kabaddi. Team maintained control from start to finish with disciplined defence and sharp raiding combinations. India shifted to a fully attacking strategy in the second half preventing any comeback from Chinese Taipei. Captain Ritu Negi and vice-captain Pushpa Rana led with tactical maturity and consistent performance.

questions.question: Which country won its fourth Davis Cup title 2025?
questions.explanation: Italy won its fourth Davis Cup title with a 2-0 victory over Spain in the final. The win also completed Italy’s third straight Davis Cup championship on home soil. Davis Cup means the World-wide International Men’s Tennis Team Championship. Matteo Berrettini won the first singles match by defeating Pablo Carreno Busta 6-3 6-4. Flavio Cobolli won the second singles match by defeating Jaume Munar 1-6 7-6(5) 7-5. Italy secured three consecutive titles in 2023, 2024 and 2025, along with its first title in 1976. Italy became the first team after the United States to achieve three back-to-back Davis Cup championships.

questions.question: Which Indian city is the host of 2030 Commonwealth Games?
questions.explanation: Ahmedabad has been formally awarded the hosting rights of the 2030 Commonwealth Games. The decision was approved by delegates from 74 Commonwealth nations at the General Assembly in Glasgow, United Kingdom. This will be the second time India hosts the Games, after New Delhi in 2010, and supports India’s ambition to host the Olympics in 2036. Ahmedabad has upgraded sports infrastructure and the 2030 Games will feature 15–17 sports, celebrating Commonwealth sporting tradition.

questions.question: Sarwagya Singh Kushwaha, who has become the youngest chess player in history to earn an official FIDE rating, belongs to which state?
questions.explanation: Sarwagya Singh Kushwaha from Sagar district, Madhya Pradesh became the youngest chess player in history to earn an official FIDE rating at 3 years, 7 months, and 20 days. He broke the previous record held by Anish Sarkar of Kolkata, who achieved it at 3 years, 8 months, and 19 days. Kushwaha is currently enrolled in nursery school and holds a rapid rating of 1,572. To earn a FIDE rating, a player must defeat at least one international opponent; Kushwaha defeated three international players in events across Madhya Pradesh and India.

questions.question: Which university topped the Khelo India University Games 2025 medal tally?
questions.explanation: The 5th Khelo India University Games concluded with a grand ceremony in Jaipur, Rajasthan. Chandigarh University, Punjab topped the medal tally, followed by Lovely Professional University (LPU), Punjab in second place and Guru Nanak Dev University in third place. The games were held from 24 November to 5 December 2025 across seven cities of Rajasthan: Jaipur, Ajmer, Udaipur, Jodhpur, Bikaner, Kota, and Bharatpur. Khelo India University Games 2025 was the fifth edition conducted under the Government of India’s Khelo India initiative.

questions.question: Which Indian city is the host of Squash World Cup 2025?
questions.explanation: Vishnu Kumar Shrivastava from Chhattisgarh has been nominated as the first-ever jury member for Squash World Cup 2025. The event is held in Chennai from 9–14 December 2025. It is organised by the Squash Rackets Federation of India (SRFI) under the World Squash Federation (WSF). Squash has recently been included in the Olympics.

questions.question: Simranpreet Kaur Brar won which medal in the women’s 25-meter pistol event at the ISSF World Cup Final 2025?
questions.explanation: Simranpreet Kaur Brar won the gold medal in the women’s 25-meter pistol event at the International Shooting Sport Federation (ISSF) World Cup Final 2025 in Doha, Qatar. She equalled the Junior World Record of the Paris Olympics champion by hitting 41 out of 50 shots in the finals. In the men’s 50m rifle 3-positions event, Aishwary Pratap Singh Tomar won the silver medal with a score of 413.3, just 0.9 points behind the gold medalist. India is second on the medal tally with six medals: two golds, three silvers, and one bronze.

questions.question: Which city is the venue of 4th Test National Cricket Championship for Deaf 2025?
questions.explanation: The Indian Deaf Cricket Association (IDCA), supported by the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) and recognized by Deaf International Cricket Council (DICC) & Asian Deaf Cricket Association, launched the 4th Test National Cricket Championship for Deaf on 18th December 2025 in Kolkata. The week-long tournament runs from 18th to 23rd December 2025. Indian Deaf Cricket Association (IDCA), founded in 2020 by Mr. Sumit Jain, governs Deaf Cricket in India, develops talent, and provides opportunities at state, national, and international levels.
